title: Paspalum calliferum
taxonomy: Paspalum calliferum is classified under the family Poaceae, commonly known as the grass family. This classification is based on its botanical characteristics and morphological features. The species is further categorized under the subfamily Panicoideae, which includes a large group of grasses. Within the subfamily, it is placed in the tribe Paniceae, a group of grasses that are typically found in tropical and subtropical regions. The specific classification of Paspalum calliferum provides valuable information about its evolutionary history and relationships with other plant species.
benefits: Paspalum calliferum is a type of grass that is commonly found in tropical and subtropical regions. Research on Paspalum calliferum reveals that it has potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. Paspalum calliferum has also been traditionally used to treat digestive issues and to reduce fever. While more research is needed to confirm its efficacy, Paspalum calliferum shows promise as a natural remedy for various health conditions.
